result.summary: Contentstack exposes multiple authenticated HTTP APIs for content, admin, analytics, launch, automation, personalization, and AI workflows, plus a GraphQL delivery API, two public delivery-style HTTP APIs (Image Delivery and Personalize Edge), and the `csdx` CLI.
result.description: Contentstack is a headless CMS and digital experience platform for managing and delivering content, personalization, launch/deployment, and related AI-powered workflows. It provides regional APIs and tooling for stack content, organization administration, analytics, automation, and front-end delivery use cases.

stack_api_key.label: Stack API key
stack_api_key.setup: In Contentstack, open your stack, then go to **Settings** → **Stack**. Under **API Credentials**, copy the stack API key. The Analytics API docs describe this path at [Analytics API](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/developers/apis/analytics-api).

delivery_token.label: Delivery token
delivery_token.setup: Log in to [Contentstack](https://www.contentstack.com/login/), open your stack, then go to **Settings** → **Tokens** → **+ Delivery Token**. Choose the branches/aliases and publishing environment, then click **Generate Token**. See [Create a Delivery Token](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/headless-cms/create-a-delivery-token) and [About Delivery Tokens](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/headless-cms/about-delivery-tokens).

management_token.label: Management token
management_token.setup: Log in to [Contentstack](https://www.contentstack.com/login/), open your stack, then go to **Settings** → **Tokens** → **Management Tokens** → **+ Management Token**. Choose scope, permissions, and expiry, then click **Generate Token** and copy the token immediately. See [Generate a Management Token](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/headless-cms/generate-a-management-token).

authtoken.label: User authtoken
authtoken.setup: Authtokens are user session tokens returned by the Contentstack login API after signing in. The API docs say to use the **Log in to your account** request under Content Management API user session to retrieve it; browser sessions can also expose it in developer tools. See [Content Management API](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/developers/apis/content-management-api), [Administration API](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/developers/apis/administration-api), and [Analytics API](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/developers/apis/analytics-api).

oauth_token.label: Contentstack OAuth access token
oauth_token.setup: Create an app in the [Developer Hub](https://app.contentstack.com/#!/developerhub), configure [Contentstack OAuth](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/developers/developer-hub/contentstack-oauth), and request the scopes needed by the target API. Contentstack issues access tokens for user apps and, for supported surfaces, app/M2M flows. Use the returned access token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

m2m_app_token.label: Machine-to-Machine app OAuth token
m2m_app_token.setup: Ask Contentstack to enable Machine-to-Machine apps for your organization (the docs mark this feature Beta). Then create/configure the M2M app in [Developer Hub](https://app.contentstack.com/#!/developerhub) and obtain an OAuth 2.0 access token with the required scopes for the API you want to call. See [Machine to Machine Apps](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/developer-hub/machine-to-machine-apps) and [Contentstack OAuth](https://www.contentstack.com/docs/developers/developer-hub/contentstack-oauth).

variables.name: stack_api_key
variables.description: The stack API key is part of the GraphQL request URL.
